sections.paragraph:
εἰσηγέομαι 1 aor. εἰσηγησάμην (Simonides, in Athen. 15, 680d cod. A [s. Diehl Fgm. 71 with Wilamowitz’s correction ἁγεῖται]; Hdt. et al.; pap; Ps 63:6 Sym.; Pseudo-Eupolemus, p. 198, 14f Denis [in Eus. PE 9, 17, 8]; Joseph., Philo, Tat.; gener. ‘lead in, introduce’) to bring to bear on a matter, display, apply πᾶσαν εἰσήγησαι σπουδήν make every effort, lose no time AcPlCor 1:16.—DELG s.v. ἡγέομαι.

aor. aor. = aorist

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Diehl Diehl , ed., s. AnthLG—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Denis Denis = AD., Fragmenta pseudepigraphorum quae supersunt Graeca—Lists 2 beg., 5, 6

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

gener. gener. = generally

AcPlCor AcPlCor = Acts of Paul: correspondence with Corinthians (a.k.a. Third Corinthians), text according to PBodmer X—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 294
